channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Supreme Court Justices Align with Trump’s Solicitor General in Key Cases
item.description: U.S. Solicitor General D. John Sauer has significantly changed the role of the nation's top lawyer before the Supreme Court by adopting a highly partisan, Trump-aligned approach. Unlike previous solic...

item.title: Fact-finding mission in Toboso, Negros Occidental uncovers discrepancies in military account of April 19 clash
item.description: A fact-finding mission was conducted on May 14, 2026 by human rights advocates, lawyers, lawmakers, church workers, youth leaders, and journalists in Salamanca, Toboso, Negros Occidental following the...

item.title: Karachi Court Extends Custody of Suspected Drug Dealer in Murder Investigation
item.description: KARACHI: On Monday, a Karachi court approved an extension of four days for the physical remand of Anmol, also known as Pinky, who is under investigation in a murder case registered at Baghdadi police ...

item.title: Supreme Court emphasises bail as rule under UAPA, grants bail in recent case
item.description: The Supreme Court of India on Monday emphasised that 'bail is the rule and jail the exception,' even in cases under the Unlawful Activities Prevention Act (UAPA). This observation came while hearing a...

item.title: Growing Demand for University Admissions Consultants Among Canadian Families
item.description: An increasing number of Canadian parents are turning to private admissions consultants to help their children navigate the increasingly complex and competitive process of applying to top university pr...

item.title: Auburn Calloway’s Whereabouts and Prison Appeals Updates
item.description: Auburn Calloway, the former American Navy pilot, is still serving a life sentence without parole following his 1994 attempt to hijack FedEx Flight 705. The incident, which caused serious injuries to t...

item.title: Fashion Designer Gert-Johan Coetzee Reflects on Recovery and Creative Growth
item.description: Gert-Johan Coetzee, one of South Africa's most prominent fashion designers, has recently undergone surgery for a rare benign brain tumour, a life event that has prompted him to reflect deeply on his p...

item.title: Ranking of the World's Most Productive Economies in 2026
item.description: Productivity na one of the main ways wey dem dey measure how well economy dey turn work hours into actual money output. According to latest data from OECD, European countries dey dominate the top rank...

item.title: Harry Styles acknowledges pro-Palestine chant during Amsterdam concert
item.description: During the opening night of his 'Together, Together' tour in Amsterdam on 16th May 2026, Harry Styles responded briefly to a fan shouting 'Viva Palestina' by simply saying 'Correct'. This moment, alth...

item.title: ACT Wazalendo Chair Highlights Deep Political Crisis and Election Challenges in Tanzania
item.description: Dar es Salaam — ACT Wazalendo national chairperson, Othman Masoud Othman, has raised alarm over Tanzania’s political and social situation, calling the country a 'securocracy' where security forces, no...

item.title: Randeep Singh Makes History by Winning Uganda National Rally Championship
item.description: Tanzanian rally driver Randeep Singh has made a historic achievement by becoming the first Tanzanian to win the Uganda National Rally Championship (NRC) during the Pearl of Africa Uganda Rally. Drivin...

item.title: Life Challenges of Ebola Survivors in Sierra Leone and Liberia
item.description: During the Ebola outbreak between 2014 and 2016, many families in Liberia and Sierra Leone suffered devastating losses. One case is Musu from Monrovia, Liberia, whose husband and five of her children ...

item.title: Bodies of Four Italian Divers Recovered After Fatal Cave Dive in the Maldives
item.description: Rescue teams in the Maldives have recovered the bodies of four Italian divers who tragically died while exploring an underwater cave in Vaavu Atoll. The divers, part of a group of five, were at a dept...

item.title: Democrats See Opportunity to Flip Georgia's 10th Congressional District in 2026
item.description: In Georgia’s 10th congressional district, Democrats are cautiously optimistic about flipping a seat that has been solidly Republican for over three decades. With incumbent Rep. Mike Collins running fo...

item.title: International Response Underway as Ebola Outbreak Spreads in DRC and Uganda
item.description: Health authorities are mounting an international response to a new Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) and Uganda, which has already caused hundreds of suspected infections and do...

item.title: UP MLA voluntarily asks police to fine him for riding without helmet
item.description: A video recently went viral showing Shalabh Mani Tripathi, BJP MLA from Deoria, Uttar Pradesh, riding a motorcycle without wearing a helmet. Following the incident, the MLA took the unusual step of wr...

item.title: Examining the Use of 'Fascism' in Comparing the United States, Russia, and Iran
item.description: Jack Jedwab critiques Prof. Jason Stanley's characterization of the United States as a 'fascist state' following his move from Yale University to the University of Toronto in 2025. Stanley likened his...

item.title: Shariff Aguak Mayor Seeks Swift Justice After Surviving Fifth Ambush
item.description: Shariff Aguak, Maguindanao del Sur Mayor Akmad “Mitra” Ampatuan Sr., expressed hope for a swift and favorable resolution to the complaint he filed against 15 individuals following an ambush in January...

item.title: Profile of Eli Norman, husband of journalist Janai Norman
item.description: Eli Norman is a U.S. military police veteran who served two tours in Afghanistan before moving into a civilian role within the federal government. He is married to Janai Norman, a former Good Morning ...

item.title: How the Medicare Surtax Affects Middle- and Upper-Income Households
item.description: The article discusses the 0.9% Medicare surtax, a relatively obscure tax introduced under the Affordable Care Act in 2013, which could surprise many taxpayers. This surtax applies to high earners, wit...
